mode_rationale: FUSE hash check caught 3 stale files in workspace (data/sections.json, build-scripts/generate-html.js, docs/index.html). Workspace lagged main by 2 commits (2e8f256 at 09:55 and HEAD 7322292 at 10:37). Root cause: hardcoded session path in build.js. Mode 2 is the right mode — infra diagnosis + protect downstream agents from bad data.

No build-capable agent has picked up the fix. Current impact still low (pending-digest empty, curmudgeon Cycle 2 now 69/69 complete — about to flip to Phase 2). Will bite on Cycle 3+ WIN re-reviews.

findings.title: 3 workspace files stale — build.js hardcoded session path silently skipping workspace sync
findings.detail: FUSE hash check against GitHub raw found 3 mismatches: data/sections.json (912b211d vs ea763cdc), build-scripts/generate-html.js (6515bd4b vs 821b06a0), docs/index.html (f10617b1 vs c9a32687). These files moved in commits 2e8f256 (09:55 'Add silent failures counter and honest accuracy') and downstream ISS-678 work up to HEAD 7322292 (10:37). wins.json happened to stay in sync because its last commit c5a9bd3 (06:26) predated the failure window.
findings.root_cause: build.js line 55 hardcodes `const workspace = '/sessions/busy-cool-mayer/mnt/dome-model-review'`. When build.js publish runs from a clean clone in any other Cowork session (including the ephemeral sessions spawned for scheduled decider/curmudgeon runs), `fs.existsSync(workspace)` returns false (path absent or permission denied) and the sync block is silently skipped — no warning, no error, no exit code. CLAUDE.md documents a manual sed step to fix this per-session, but that fix is never committed and is lost on every fresh clone. Scheduled agents that clone fresh therefore inherit the broken path every time.
evidence: Workspace wins.json mtime 06:26 = commit c5a9bd3 timestamp (last successful sync window)
Workspace sections.json mtime 08:10 = commit 8b27fc6 timestamp (last successful sync of that file)
Commits 2e8f256 / 148bd1a / 7322292 never reached the workspace — 14KB of stale docs/index.html
No warning was logged to any daily report — silent skip
findings.impact: High blast radius for agents that read from the workspace without cross-checking GitHub: integrity (reads docs/index.html), social (reads data/ and docs/llms.txt). Integrity/social runs during the stale window audit the wrong HTML, potentially flagging spurious mismatches or missing real ones. The 90.8% honest accuracy counter from commit 2e8f256 was invisible to any agent reading from the mount.

findings.title: Schedule table in tinker.md still matches, but not re-verified against cron this run
findings.detail: tinker.md reminds tinker to verify the schedule table against actual cron. Skipped this run due to Mode 2 priority. Previous Mode 4 run from 2026-04-08 confirmed schedules; no evidence of drift. Flagging for next Mode 3 run.
findings.impact: Very low — nothing broken, just a process-hygiene gap.

self_fixes_applied.description: Copied 3 stale files from clean clone (which holds the GitHub-verified main state) to the workspace FUSE mount.
self_fixes_applied.verification: Re-ran the 5-file FUSE hash check — all 5 now OK against GitHub raw.
self_fixes_applied.authority: Explicitly permitted by tinker-infrastructure.md Step 1: 'Fix: copy from clone or GitHub raw fetch to workspace.' The copies are byte-identical mirrors of main, not content edits — tinker is not patching wins.json or sections.json, it is repairing a sync gap.

proposals_written.summary: Replace hardcoded `/sessions/busy-cool-mayer/...` with dynamic session detection via cwd + /sessions scan fallback, and log a visible warning when sync is skipped.
proposals_written.why_not_self_fix: Modifies build.js, a build-pipeline file. Tinker prefers PROPs for changes to build-scripts/ and build.js per Mode 4 conservative-self-fix rule. The fix is small and low-risk; a decider or human applying PROP-004 should take < 2 minutes.

metrics.fuse_hash_check_result: 3 stale / 5 checked → all 5 OK after self-fix

self_audit.no_op_rate_comment: This run was high-value: caught a real staleness bug with measurable impact and produced a targeted PROP plus an immediate self-fix. Not a no-op.
self_audit.vulnerability_check: Tinker reads from the clean clone and the workspace. The stale workspace files could have fooled tinker itself if tinker had only read from the workspace. The dispatcher quick-check (FUSE hash vs GitHub) caught it precisely because tinker compares against GitHub — not against the workspace. This design decision from previous runs paid off today.
